name: celebrate
description: 🎆 Stop hook that fires a fireworks celebration when the ACTIVE /domain's progress crosses to 100% (a 'novel goal' reached). On every turn-end it resolves the active domain (same convention as the `domain` skill — git repo root + `$HOME/.claude/domain-active.tsv` active pointer, else project basename; snapshot path via the repo's `DOMAINS.tape` roster) and counts `- [ ]`/`- [x]` milestones in the active DOMAIN.md (done/total; 100% = total>0 && done==total). It keeps the last-seen percent per domain in `$HOME/.sidecar/celebrate/<domain-key>.seen` and fires ONLY on the crossing (prev <100 → now =100), then records 100 so it never re-fires while it stays at 100; if it drops below 100 and re-reaches, it celebrates again (natural). On the crossing the hook fires TWO in-session-only effects: (1) emits a multi-line ASCII fireworks burst + '🎆 <DOMAIN> @goal 100% 달성!' as additionalContext into the chat, and (2) rings the terminal bell (`printf '\a'`). No animation, no separate window. HARD CONSTRAINT: the Claude Code TUI canvas is owned by Ink, so this hook NEVER writes ANSI/animation to /dev/tty (that would corrupt the screen) — the in-stream ASCII burst is static text and the bell is a bare BEL char. UNCONDITIONAL (commons @D s11 — no env-var/flag/config escape hatch): the ONLY gates are real preconditions — an active domain exists AND has ≥1 milestone AND progress just crossed 100; no active domain / 0 milestones / not crossing → silent exit 0. Implemented in hexa-lang (`_celebrate.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`); reads stdin via `exec("cat")` (avoids the hexa fake-FILE segfault), checks path existence via shell `test -e`, portable `$HOME`/`${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` paths only.
